I notice some interesting 1-pixel blue (b) objects at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. These form a pattern that looks like a checkerboard/diagonal pattern. Let me look at this area more carefully.
Looking at the pattern:
- Row 34: b at col 9, c at col 10, b at col 11
- Row 35: b at col 8, c at col 9, b at col 10
- Row 36: b at col 9, c at col 10, b at col 11
- Row 37: b at col 8, c at col 9, b at col 10
This is a 4x3 area with blue pixels in a checkerboard pattern. This is below the red/dark red blocks and to the left of the gray block with green.
Wait, let me reconsider the layout. Let me look at the ascii more carefully around this area.
Actually, looking at the ascii from before:
Row 30: ccccccccggggRRRRRRrrrrcccccccccc...
Row 31: ccccccccggggRRRRRRrrrrcccccccccc...
Row 32: ccccccccggggRRRRRRrrrrcccccccccc...
Row 33: ccccccccggggRRRRRRrrrrcccccccccc...
Row 34: ccccccccbcbccccccccccccccccccc...
Row 35: ccccccccbcbccccccccccccccccc...
Row 36: cccccccccbcbcccccccccccccccc...
Row 37: ccccccccbcbccccccccccccccccc...
Row 38: ccccccccggggggcccccccccccccccc...
So below the red/dark red blocks (rows 30-33), there's a pattern of b-c-b (blue-charcoal-blue) at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. This could be a "connector" or "wire" pattern.
Let me try arrow keys to see if there's a cursor that moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try RIGHT to see if there's a cursor that moves
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print("Action result:", last_action_result)

# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

print("\nChanges:")
for nid in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn is None:
        print(f"  New node id={nid}: color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}")
    elif an is None:
        print(f"  Removed node id={nid}")
    elif bn['color'] != an['color']:
        print(f"  Color change id={nid}: {bn['color']} -> {an['color']}")
    elif bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
        print(f"  Pixels change id={nid}: {bn['pixels']} -> {an['pixels']}")
    elif bn['hash'] != an['hash']:
        print(f"  Hash change id={nid}: {bn['hash']} -> {an['hash']}")
    elif bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
        print(f"  Boundary change id={nid}: {bn['boundary']} -> {an['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
